replication_study.hypothesis: Covid-19 verbal jokes will be rated as more aversive (higher mean score on the 0-to-6 scale) than non-pandemic verbal jokes in an independent online sample.

data_plan.source_type: Online self-administered survey exported from formR/Qualtrics
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Full cleaned dataset; two parts merged on anonymous participant_id
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Participant (mean aversiveness across items within each joke category)
data_plan.access_details: Open de-identified CSVs supplied in replication_data folder.

qualification.explanation: The files contain the same set of humor stimuli and identical rating scales used in the original Italian lockdown study, allowing a direct test of the focal comparison on a fresh sample collected under comparable online procedures.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Stimuli file names (pic###_disturbing) and 7-point aversiveness scale match description in original paper (original_paper.pdf, pp. 4-5) and post_registration.json (outcome_variables).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Sample is drawn mainly from U.S. states (state column with values such as "CO", see replication_data/Bischetti_Survey_Part1_deidentify.csv) and collected in Jan-2021 instead of Mar-Apr-2020 during the Italian lockdown (post_registration.json data section). Thus it provides an independent population/time point.

data_plan.notes: Stimulus-to-category mapping Excel files were not included; a stimulus_metadata.csv placeholder is expected. If absent, the Python code infers categories heuristically. Any missing aversiveness cells are list-wise dropped.

planned_method.steps:
1. Load the two de-identified CSV files.
2. Merge on participant_id to create one wide respondent file.
3. Select aversiveness columns (suffix _disturbing) and convert to long format.
4. Attach stimulus category labels (covid-verbal vs non-verbal) via metadata file or heuristic rule.
5. Compute each participant’s mean aversiveness for the two categories.
6. Conduct paired t-test on the within-participant difference (covid − non-covid).
7. Save summary (means, t, p) to /app/data/replication_summary.csv.
planned_method.models: Paired samples t-test; supplementary descriptive stats. (Optional mixed-effects extension mirrors original lmer).
planned_method.outcome_variable: Mean Aversiveness rating (0–6) per participant per joke type
planned_method.independent_variables: Joke Type (Covid verbal vs Non-Covid verbal)
planned_method.control_variables: None for focal paired test; demographic covariates could be explored separately.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Mean paired difference in aversiveness (β_hat = M_covid − M_non)

planned_method.missing_data_handling: List-wise deletion of NA aversiveness ratings before aggregation.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Reject H0 if p < 0.05 and signed difference is positive; otherwise fail to replicate.

files.replication_data/replicate_aversion.py: Standalone Python script that loads, merges, reshapes data; attaches stimulus labels; performs paired t-test; prints and saves replication_summary.csv.

codebase.notes: Script searches for data files in /app/data (or /app/data/replication_data) to satisfy container path constraint. Heuristic labelling executes only if formal metadata file is missing.

analysis.instructions: Inside the container, run:  python replication_data/replicate_aversion.py   (DATA_DIR defaults to /app/data).  Inspect replication_summary.csv for results.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Mean aversiveness difference (covid − non), t-statistic, p-value, directionality compared against original effect (+0.39, t=2.26, p<.05).
